steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The noun phrase on the left side ends with a prepositional phrase (“like the Harbor Park bicycle”) that identifies the specific noun. On the right side is a relative clause starting with “which,” positioned before the main verb of the sentence.

steps.title: Apply the rule for non-restrictive relative clauses
steps.content:
The relative clause “which is about 0.38 miles long” provides additional information about a noun that is already specifically identified (“the Harbor Park bicycle path”). Such non-restrictive clauses must be set off from the rest of the sentence by commas. When a non-restrictive clause appears in the middle of a sentence, it needs both a comma before it and a comma after it. The other three options fail to include both required commas, violating this rule.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
